description: Methodology page — rebuilt around the Signal Intelligence framework (PR #646). The substantive page body lives in MethodologyBody.jsx (rendered by both the /#readme legacy route and the v2 Methodology page, so the surfaces cannot drift); MethodologyPage.jsx is a thin hero wrapper. The body is plain-English prose: the question the framework answers, the two-layer architecture (three volatility triggers + one seven-indicator cycle composite), the four-state read, how thresholds are set, honest limits, then a section per other site page, then a live Sources registry. Fixture updated 2026-05-21 — the prior fixture described the retired v10-allocator / headline-gauge design.
required_section_headings: When to take chips off the table.
Two layers of evidence, one regime read.
Three volatility triggers, ordered by who cracks first.
One cycle composite, built from seven indicators.
One label that combines both layers.
required_source_attribution_pattern: registered against a vendor and a freshness window
required_drilldown_targets: Jump-nav chips → in-page section scroll (scrollToSection, no URL-hash mutation)

_meta.ux_designer_signoff: approved — fixture re-anchored to live Signal Intelligence page
_meta.senior_quant_signoff: approved — triggers / composite / four-state copy matches engine
_meta.lead_developer_uat: checker green; vite build clean; Chrome MCP UAT light + dark

approved_lexicon_vocabulary._note: #1159 — council-approved two-axis lexicon. required_lexicon_states above lists only the states this page actually renders as visible text.
